zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
tests: Fix grep syntax

In basic regular expressions | is not a special character that spearates
branches, so use an extended regular expression.
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -Eq '^not ok|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
'make test': Fix breakage introduced by 4d3da30f8b726c6e0eeb3adda06adc9eaa329b3b:
test failure would not be reflected by the exit code of 'make'.

Setting a shell parameter in the left-hand side of a pipe is not visible to
commands after the pipe, because the left-hand side forks.  (That's true both
in 'sh' used by 'make' and in 'zsh' that runs tests/test-highlighting.zsh, at
least on my system.)  Therefore, move the colorizing hook to where it doesn't
interfere with setting the $something_failed (in tests/test-highlighting.zsh)
and $result (in Makefile) parameters.
removed_lines: -  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message: test harness: Exit 1 if a 'todo' test passed.
removed_lines: -  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep '^not ok' | grep -qv ' # TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
